danger_signals.visual_form: Animated spheres bouncing off walls and room surfaces
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; ball behind glove = life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Entities that occupy room space and move autonomously
danger_signals.meaning: Direct combat threat; some enemies deal one-hit knockouts in later worlds

danger_signals.visual_form: Walls with partially cleared tile grids showing gaps and cleared sections
danger_signals.meaning: Progression indicator; impassable areas until fully cleared
Health status; 5 ball losses = 1 life loss; 0 lives = game over

danger_signals.visual_form: Energy ball with altered visual appearance while holding bonus active
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate self-damage risk if held too long during bomb effect

safety_signals.visual_form: Tiles marked with '?' symbol on walls
safety_signals.meaning: Potential power-up sources; interactive reward tiles

safety_signals.visual_form: Red directional arrow overlay on wall
safety_signals.meaning: Cleared wall passage; safe passage to next room

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual representations of LIFE, EXTRA, ICE BALL, BOMB BALL, POWER, ROW, SUPER BALL, TRIPLE, ROBO-BULLET REFILL, PINBALL
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary beneficial effects or resource restoration
Resource for tile destruction and enemy attack; one-hit tile destruction capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Specially colored moving tile among wall tiles
safety_signals.meaning: Unlocks all adjacent room pathways when hit; escape mechanism

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: First-person perspective centered on glove; glove as game avatar
Trapped in dimensional maze; narrative objective, not visually present in gameplay

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy difficulty increases across three worlds; later worlds feature one-hit-kill variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mobile entities inhabiting maze rooms; visual distinction by world progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 3D first-person perspective showing rectangular tiled walls (20 tiles per side wall, 32 per top/bottom, 40 on far wall)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Energy ball spawns and begins bouncing; player must engage with tile destruction puzzle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wall tiles incrementally disappear as hit by balls or Robo-Bullets; grid pattern shows gaps
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Walls become passable only when fully cleared; red arrow indicates valid exit direction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive worlds (1-3) show increasing enemy toughness and harder-to-hit tile placements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies deal greater damage; certain tiles positioned in less accessible wall locations; random warping increases threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person perspective maze environment set in 'outer space' (2005 futuristic setting)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Room-to-room navigation; player must map 60+ rooms in world 2 to avoid disorientation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ball disappears behind glove; new ball spawns; life meter segment depletes after 5 ball losses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game over when all lives depleted; no continues available

environmental_storytelling.example: Setting established as year 2005 with shuttle commander trapped in dimensional maze in outer space; context explains puzzle-solving purpose
Rescue mission mechanic: throwing energy balls at walls = rescue action; maze progression = journey through dimensional space; three worlds = escalating danger
Each room has numeric identifier enabling player map-making; encourages spatial reasoning and exploration documentation
Question mark tiles signal reward opportunities; different power-up effects communicated through icon/effect type (ICE BALL, BOMB BALL, SUPER BALL convey function through naming)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Bounces off walls and tiles; destroyed if passes behind glove; AI ball loss triggers new spawn
Variable threat level; some deal standard damage
One-hit knockouts possible; increased aggression and movement difficulty
Blocks passage; destroyed by energy ball or Robo-Bullet impact
Triggers power-up reward when hit; indicates interactive environment
Points valid exit direction; indicates passable threshold to adjacent room
Controlable via Power Glove or standard controller; hits/throws/punches ball; shoots Robo-Bullets from index finger

notes: Super Glove Ball communicates world-state primarily through mechanical and visual feedback rather than faction-based systems. The game lacks traditional political or factional conflict; danger/safety signals are gameplay-mechanic focused (ball physics, tile states, enemy behavior). The dimensional maze setting and first-person perspective create environmental storytelling through spatial layout and progression challenges. World state escalation occurs through difficulty curve (enemy toughness, tile placement complexity) rather than narrative beats. Power-up system provides tactical resource management signals through icon recognition and effect types.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor) - Super Glove Ball article
